title: Firmographic Targeting - The Basics That Most Skip

questions.question: Why do we start targeting with Firmographics before Psychographics?

options.text: Because they are more important for closing the deal
Because they describe the emotional state of the buyer
Because they are 'filterable' in tools like LinkedIn Sales Navigator
Because investors require them

questions.explanation: You can search for 'Headcount: 50' (Firmographic) in a database. You cannot search for 'Feeling anxious about Q4' (Psychographic). Firmographics allow you to build the initial list.

questions.question: Which of these is a Specific Vertical (Good) vs. a Generic Industry (Bad)?

options.text: Generic: 'Healthcare' vs. Specific: 'Hospitals'
Generic: 'E-commerce' vs. Specific: 'DTC Skincare Brands'
Generic: 'SaaS' vs. Specific: 'Tech Companies'
Generic: 'Finance' vs. Specific: 'Banks'

questions.explanation: 'E-commerce' is too broad—it includes Amazon and a teenager selling stickers. 'DTC Skincare Brands' implies specific inventory, marketing, and regulatory challenges you can speak to.

questions.question: Why is 'Growth Stage' often more important than 'Company Size'?

options.text: It determines their office location
It indicates their willingness to burn cash vs. focus on profit
It tells you how many employees they have
It doesn't matter, size is the only metric

questions.explanation: A bootstrapped company (Profit mode) hates recurring costs. A Series B company (Growth mode) loves tools that help them scale fast, even if expensive. They buy differently.

questions.question: What is 'Technographic' targeting?

options.text: Targeting based on the CTO's age
Targeting based on the software and tools the company already uses
Targeting based on how technical the product is
Targeting IT departments only

questions.explanation: Technographics identify the tech stack (e.g., 'Must use Salesforce'). This can create a perfect entry point or 'wedge' for your product.

questions.question: What is the 'SMB Trap' described in the lesson?

options.text: Targeting small businesses that cannot afford your product
Using government census categories like 'SMB' which group unrelated businesses together
Selling to companies with less than 10 employees
Focusing only on local businesses in your city

questions.explanation: The 'SMB Trap' is treating 'Small and Medium Business' as a target market. It is a statistical category that groups disparate businesses (pizza shops vs. tech startups) that have nothing in common.

questions.question: List the 3 most important firmographic criteria for YOUR ideal customer (industry, company size, geography). Explain why each matters for your specific product or service.
questions.promptForAI: Evaluate if the firmographic criteria are specific enough. Check for: (1) clear industry definition, (2) quantified company size, (3) geographic rationale. Suggest ways to make criteria more precise.
